page: Amazon Web Services m6idn.2xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8375C processor operating at 3.5 GHz. The configuration provides 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It includes 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled as a 474 GB NVMe SSD instance store, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 12 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place the instance consistently in the average tier. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng score 1891.79 and 7723.27 respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show 417.3 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 57.49 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. In application-specific tests, the instance achieves 7.96 million operations per second on Redis SET operations and 11.9 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-model LLM inference show weaker performance, with text generation at 7.67 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m6idn.2xlarge offers a balanced resource density, combining dedicated Intel Xeon compute, DDR4 memory, and high-speed local NVMe storage without requiring separate storage attachments. This configuration is suitable for general-purpose workloads, including database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and moderate caching layers like Redis.
description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.2xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz, offering 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. The hardware profile includes a bundled 474 GB NVMe SSD local instance store and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate average performance across most categories, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 6090.0 and 7.96 million operations per second in Redis SET tests. However, it exhibits lower performance in multi-threaded decompression and medium-model LLM inference. This instance provides a balanced resource-to-cost profile for workloads requiring local high-speed storage and moderate compute, making it suitable for database operations, web serving, and general-purpose applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.2xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR4 RAM, a 474 GB NVMe SSD, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering balanced mid-tier benchmark performance.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed local NVMe storage, and optimized network performance.

32.0 GB of DDR4 memory paired with 474 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS and network-heavy tasks
Consistent mid-tier performance in database operations and static web serving
Suitable for general-purpose applications, web servers, and database hosting
